seoTitle: Dark Corner vs Darkwood — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Dark Corner and Darkwood. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Dark Corner vs Darkwood: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Dark Corner and Darkwood.
heading: Dark Corner vs Darkwood — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Dark Corner, New South Wales and Darkwood, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Dark Corner has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Darkwood (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Dark Corner or Darkwood safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Dark Corner has a flood risk score of 35/100, while Darkwood scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Dark Corner scores 44/100 for bushfire risk. Darkwood scores 22/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Dark Corner or Darkwood?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Dark Corner has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.35% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
